title: Can you pilot an angel
wikitext:
[]: Can you pilot an angel             Quote Post by hwdge » Sat Dec 01, 2012 3:34 am So i've been wondering ever since i saw tunniel in 2 22 , can you pilot an angel? Tunniel has this entry plug thing attached to it for some unexplained reason and also since the evas (minus 01 maybe) and angels all come from adam, they might be able to transplant parts from one to another like how eva 01 steals zeruel's left arm after it's been torn off. So what do you all think?

[]: Re: Can you pilot an angel             Quote Post by Tribblepoo » Sat Dec 01, 2012 3:50 am hwdge wrote: So i've been wondering ever since i saw tunniel in 2 22 , can you pilot an angel? Tunniel has this entry plug thing attached to it for some unexplained reason and also since the evas (minus 01 maybe) and angels all come from adam, they might be able to transplant parts from one to another like how eva 01 steals zeruel's left arm after it's been torn off. So what do you all think?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Dec 01, 2012 8:01 am As goofy as the idea is, I had to smile a little at the prospect of joyriding in a Ramiel-clone of my very own.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sun Dec 30, 2012 2:24 pm We already have massive weaponized synthetic humanoid constructs. Are you not entertained?

[]:             Quote Post by amitakartok » Sun Dec 30, 2012 3:05 pm The way I see it, controlling an Angel IS possible but you can't subdue it once it goes berserk because it's not running off a battery. In the manga, once Unit 01 finished nomming Zeruel, they had to tie the Eva up using net launchers because they couldn't shut her down until she decided she had enough fun for now. An Angel would never go dormant until reaching Lilith so it would invariably be a one-use weapon that would turn on you whenever it damn well wanted to. Sounds useful but is actually quite impractical.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Dec 31, 2012 12:19 am Part of the reason Eva piloting works at all is because Evas and Lilin are physically similar. Humanoid body plan with a similar nervous system and all that. Synchronization quite explicitly involves the Eva's CNS, and using LCL as a medium for transmitting nerve impulses. Angels basically throw their biology out the window and be whatever the hell they want. Erect body posture? Four limbs? Brain? Spinal column? All optional! If the Angel decided to hold onto at least a CNS, then piloting MIGHT be possible, but everything but practical or even desirable. Just synching with the humanoid Evas causes significant strain on the pilots' nervous systems. Trying to synch with something like Tunniel would probably cause permanent brain damage or something. :p

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Mon Dec 31, 2012 12:27 am Reichu wrote: Part of the reason Eva piloting works at all is because Evas and Lilin are physically similar. Humanoid body plan with a similar nervous system and all that. Synchronization quite explicitly involves the Eva's CNS, and using LCL as a medium for transmitting nerve impulses. Angels basically throw their biology out the window and be whatever the hell they want. Erect body posture? Four limbs? Brain? Spinal column? All optional! If the Angel decided to hold onto at least a CNS, then piloting MIGHT be possible, but everything but practical or even desirable. Just synching with the humanoid Evas causes significant strain on the pilots' nervous systems. Trying to synch with something like Tunniel would probably cause permanent brain damage or something. :p

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Mon Dec 31, 2012 12:35 am CorporalChaos wrote: Then how do you explain Mari synching with Unit-05 or whatever it is in Rebuild?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Dec 31, 2012 12:37 am CorporalChaos wrote: Then how do you explain Mari synching with Unit-05 or whatever it is in Rebuild?
